title: card.rs: image_url stored unsanitized; fetch_card_image only guards https scheme
description:
LOCATION: src/card.rs:196, 217

FINDING: In fetch_and_store, meta.image_url (extracted from remote OG meta tags via parse_og_meta) is stored directly to the database at line 196 without any URL scheme validation. The fetch_card_image function checks starts_with("https://") before fetching, so the fetch itself is safe. However, the raw image_url value (which could be "javascript:...", "data:...", or a relative path) is persisted to the cards table and later included in AP Note objects served from get_card_for_post (line 298 of card.rs). A remotely-controlled site can set og:image to "javascript:alert(1)" and it will be stored and returned in the ActivityPub preview Link object. AP clients that render card previews may process this value.

IMPACT: Stored malicious URL in the cards.image_url column, propagated to AP Note objects delivered to followers. Clients that render link card images by treating the image_url as a clickable link could expose users to javascript: URI clicks.
close_reason: Fixed: image_url validated as https:// before DB storage
